title: CRDs, Controllers, Operators, Admission Policies, Webhooks, and Extension Trust · Independent Platform Trial
summary: An independent delivery team receives an unfamiliar namespace, failure domain, and policy constraint and must transfer crds, controllers, operators, admission policies, webhooks, and extension trust into a new trusted extension API and controller contract. Planned artifact: Produce an independent changed-context version of trusted extension API and controller contract with automated and judgment evidence. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debug…
objectives: Design CRD names, scope, versions, structural OpenAPI schemas, defaulting, validation, pruning, status subresource, scale, storage version, and conversion.
Build or evaluate controllers from watches, work queues, level-based reconciliation, ownership, idempotency, finalizers, status, retries, leader election, and rate limits.
Choose built-in admission, ValidatingAdmissionPolicy, validating webhook, or mutating webhook from expressiveness, failure policy, ordering, timeout, availability, and audit needs.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# kubernetes-basics cumulative orchestration portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic manifest and evidence review only.
# They never invoke kubectl, read kubeconfig, contact a cluster API, or execute host commands.
workspace: kubernetes
version-baseline:
  kubernetes: 1.36.2
  minikube: 1.38.1
  gateway-api: 1.5.1
  helm: 4.2.2
transfer-boundary: authorized-disposable-cluster
evidence:

steps.title: Commit to the mission model · Lab K8s Crd Schema Status Conversion
steps.instruction:
lab case — A lab CRD changes schema without conversion, a webhook fails closed during outage, and an operator hot-loops while fighting field ownership. Bound extension API, defaulting, validation, conversion, admission availability, reconciliation, and uninstall. Competency probe: Design CRD names, scope, versions, structural OpenAPI schemas, defaulting, validation, pruning, status subresource, scale, storage version, and conversion.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: Commit to the mission model · Lab K8s Crd Schema Status Conversion.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for design crd names, scope, versions, structural openapi schemas, defaulting, validation, pruning, status subresource, scale, storage version, and conversion. For Commit to the mission model · Lab K8s Crd Schema Status Conversion, keep K8s Crd Schema Status Conversion connected to mechanically reviewable Kubernetes object, status, and transfer evidence.

content.text: A lab CRD changes schema without conversion, a webhook fails closed during outage, and an operator hot-loops while fighting field ownership. Bound extension API, defaulting, validation, conversion, admission availability, reconciliation, and uninstall. Competency probe: Design CRD names, scope, versions, structural OpenAPI schemas, defaulting, validation, pruning, status subresource, scale, storage version, and conversion.

content.title: Commit before feedback
content.text: Choose from the stated data before opening the worked model. Record which K8s Crd Schema Status Conversion condition decided your prediction.

content.label: Authority to consult after committing
content.value: Kubernetes Architecture, Objects, and Cluster Administration — Controls control-plane and node components, API and etcd boundaries, reconciliation, object identity and status, namespaces, ownership, cluster lifecycle, conformance, and recovery.

hints: For Commit to the mission model · Lab K8s Crd Schema Status Conversion, name the context, namespace, API identity, desired state, observed status, controller, policy, and failure-domain boundary, K8s Crd Schema Status Conversion precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Commit to the mission model · Lab K8s Crd Schema Status Conversion, trace this concrete input and predict admission, defaulting, scheduling, reconciliation, status, traffic, storage, policy, disruption, and cleanup evidence before reviewing it: A lab CRD changes schema without conversion, a webhook fails closed during outage, and an operator hot-loops while fighting field ownership. Bound extension API, defaulting, validation, conversion, admission availability, reconciliation, and uninstall. Competency probe: Design CRD names, scope, versions, structural OpenAPI schemas, defaulting, validation, pruning, status subresource, scale, storage version, and conversion.

For Commit to the mission model · Lab K8s Crd Schema Status Conversion, challenge the documented misconception with object, condition, event, policy, failure, repair, rollback, and transfer evidence: A CRD automatically supplies a controller and preserves unknown fields and old versions forever.

options.text: A CRD automatically supplies a controller and preserves unknown fields and old versions forever. That claim does not survive the commit to the mission model evidence boundary.

For commit to the mission model, copy one familiar manifest or kubectl command, omit context, API discovery, desired-versus-observed status, failure, rollback, and disposable-cluster transfer checks, then accept an Applied response as production proof.
State the K8s Crd Schema Status Conversion preconditions, preserve earlier contracts, and name the trace or changed-input result that will decide success.

steps.title: Extend the cumulative artifact · Lab K8s Extension Upgrade Trust
steps.instruction:
Append the Kubernetes evidence item headed "  # Evidence: k8s-extension-upgrade-trust-1elevq". Tailor its extension-contract.yaml change to the current stakeholder case, retain a deterministic API or manifest review command, reject one concrete failure, and record a repair. The browser analyzes text only; run kubectl, Helm, kubeadm, and cluster commands later in an authorized disposable transfer environment.

Evidence target: Extend the cumulative artifact · Lab K8s Extension Upgrade Trust.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns K8s Extension Upgrade Trust into observable performance evidence. For Extend the cumulative artifact · Lab K8s Extension Upgrade Trust, keep K8s Extension Upgrade Trust connected to mechanically reviewable Kubernetes object, status, and transfer evidence.

content.code:
# Evidence: k8s-extension-upgrade-trust-1elevq
  - competency: k8s-extension-upgrade-trust
    artifact: extension-contract.yaml
    decision: extension upgrade trust
    environment: rootless development cluster with an empty namespace
    fault-injection: delete one ready Pod during a configuration rollout
    retained-proof: Pod state previous exit endpoint readiness and request result
    case-key: 1elevq
    change: |
      crd: structural-schema-with-status
      storage-version: v1
      conversion: tested
      controller: idempotent-rate-limited-reconcile
      finalizer: external-state-owned
      admission: validating-policy-first
      webhook: bounded-fail-policy
      uninstall: dependency-ordered
    verify: kubectl get crd,validatingadmissionpolicy,validatingwebhookconfiguration -o json
    failure: Reject schema-free CRDs, event-script controllers, unbounded webhooks, fail-open-by-default, or operator deletion as external-state cleanup.
    repair: Version and validate the API, test conversion, bound reconciliation and admission, preserve status, contain failure, and rehearse upgrade and uninstall.
content.caption: Shape of the required increment; replace generic language with the current stakeholder case.

content.title: Evidence is more than syntax
content.text: The structural check is only one gate. The following changed-case decision and explanation must also hold.

Competency probe: Evaluate extension provenance, permissions, webhooks, CRDs, conversion, compatibility, backup, canary, observability, failure containment, and uninstall safety.
